prompt:
We keep serializing filter state into the URL by hand and every screen does it slightly differently. I want one shared query-string codec, qs.ts, exporting encode(obj) and decode(str).

What it has to handle (the acceptance tests in test_qs.ts are the contract, run them with `node --test test_qs.ts`):

- flat pairs, obviously, with proper percent-encoding both ways; decode should also accept '+' as a space in values since some of our older links use that
- nested objects via bracket keys: {user: {name: 'ada'}} <-> user[name]=ada, to any depth
- arrays via empty brackets: {tags: ['x','y']} <-> tags[]=x&tags[]=y — a single tags[]=only must still come back as an array
- repeated plain keys collect into an array on decode (id=1&id=2 -> {id: ['1','2']})
- key segments get percent-encoded individually, brackets stay literal separators (user[first%20name]=ada)
- decode tolerates a leading '?' and a bare key with no '=' reads as empty string
- encode accepts number/boolean leaves and stringifies them; decode always yields strings, and decode(encode(x)) must round-trip structurally

No dependencies, TypeScript, node's built-in runner. Don't touch the test file.
